title: `VARHIDDEN` warning (maybe) should not apply to non-static class fields
body:
It seems that the warning [VARHIDDEN](https://verilator.org/guide/latest/warnings.html#:~:text=of%20the%20error.-,VARHIDDEN,-Warns%20that%20a) does not work quite correctly with classes.

The description of the rule says

> thus hiding the upper variable from being able to be used

which is not entirely true for classes (more precisely, objects), where we can access the shaded **field** through `this`.

Matching a field name and a parameter in a constructor is such a common pattern that some languages (e.g. [Dart](https://dart.dev/language/constructors#initializing-formal-parameters)) add syntactic sugar for it.

Example:

```systemverilog
class example;
  bit my_var;

  function new(bit my_var);
    this.my_var = my_var;
    my_var = this.my_var;
  endfunction
endclass
```

Command:

```bash
verilator --binary -j 0 -Wall example.sv
```

Output:

```text
%Warning-VARHIDDEN: example.sv:4:20: Declaration of signal hides declaration in upper scope: 'my_var'
    4 |   function new(bit my_var);
      |                    ^~~~~~
                    example.sv:2:7: ... Location of original declaration
    2 |   bit my_var;
      |       ^~~~~~
                    ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/VARHIDDEN?v=5.016
                    ... Use "/* verilator lint_off VARHIDDEN */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

---

**Actually, a suggestion**: maybe don't show this warning for non-static fields, since they are reachable via `this`?

---

*Version*: `Verilator v5.020`
